title: Project CLAUDE.md
description:
## Context
Foundation file for the project. Defines project identity, rules, and registers the autopilot workflow.

## File Scope
- `CLAUDE.md` (project root)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Defines project purpose: autonomous agent workflow loop for Claude Code
- [ ] Registers local .claude/ agents: proposer, challenger, evaluator
- [ ] Registers local .claude/ skills: discussion, autopilot-loop
- [ ] Registers /autopilot command
- [ ] Documents .autopilot/ directory purpose
- [ ] References existing global skills (planning, orchestrator) that are reused
- [ ] Sets coding conventions for config files (markdown + YAML frontmatter)

title: Claude Super Auto: Autonomous Agent Workflow Loop
close_reason: All 8 task beads complete, review passed

title: Discussion skill — adversarial debate protocol
description:
## Context
The Discussion skill orchestrates the adversarial debate between Proposer and Challenger. It acts as mediator — spawning agents, parsing messages, detecting convergence, and synthesizing results.

## Spike Learnings (from .spikes/discussion-protocol/)
- Fixed 3 rounds max, early-exit on convergence
- Sequential per round: spawn Proposer → wait → spawn Challenger → wait → check convergence
- Convergence: agreement_ratio >= 0.8 OR open_questions == 0 OR round 3 completes
- Synthesis: KEEP→include, surviving ADD→include, MODIFY→last version, undefended CUT→drop, disputed→[DISPUTED]
- For iteration 2+: seed message includes evaluator score, feedback, completed/remaining items
- Fuzzy item matching: normalize lowercase, Jaccard similarity or LLM canonicalization
- Reject and retry once if agent violates message format

## File Scope
- `.claude/skills/discussion/SKILL.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] YAML frontmatter: name, description
- [ ] Seed message template for iteration 1 and iteration 2+
- [ ] Round-by-round flow documented (spawn proposer → spawn challenger → parse → convergence check)
- [ ] Convergence detection algorithm with agreement_ratio and open_questions metrics
- [ ] Synthesis procedure: rules for KEEP/ADD/CUT/MODIFY/DISPUTED items
- [ ] Output: writes .autopilot/iteration-N/discussion-result.md
- [ ] Error handling: format violation → one retry with correction prompt
- [ ] Uses Agent Mail: send_message, fetch_inbox, summarize_thread
close_reason: Discussion skill created with full adversarial debate protocol

title: Challenger agent definition
description:
## Context
The Challenger agent contracts scope during the adversarial discussion phase. It pokes holes, simplifies, cuts features, and argues for minimalism.

## Spike Learnings (from .spikes/discussion-protocol/)
- Must use structured message format with [KEEP], [ADD], [CUT], [MODIFY] tagged items
- Must include ## Confidence, ## Concessions, ## Open Questions sections
- Communicates via Agent Mail thread `discussion-{iteration}`
- Reads proposer's message before responding each round

## File Scope
- `.claude/agents/challenger.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] YAML frontmatter: name, description, model (sonnet for cost efficiency)
- [ ] Instructions to read Agent Mail thread for context
- [ ] Strict message format template embedded in instructions
- [ ] Role: cut scope, challenge feasibility, argue for simplification
- [ ] Must include Concessions section (critical for convergence)
- [ ] Must explicitly evaluate proposer's items against success criteria

title: .autopilot/ state schema
description:
## Context
Defines the .autopilot/ directory structure and initialization logic. This is the state layer that persists across iterations.

## Spike Learnings
- Adapts .ccu/ pattern from session-state skill
- Ephemeral state (gitignored): STATUS.md, iteration working files
- Persistent state (committed): config.md, iteration summaries, evaluation results

## File Scope
- Documented within autopilot-loop SKILL.md (no separate file — schema is defined in the controller)
- Creates runtime directory structure

## Directory Schema
```
.autopilot/
  config.md                    — idea, success criteria, constraints, thresholds
  STATUS.md                    — current iteration, latest scores, state
  iteration-1/
    discussion-result.md       — synthesized discussion output
    evaluation.json            — evaluator scores
    summary.md                 — iteration summary
  iteration-2/
    ...
  FINAL.md                     — written when loop terminates
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema documented in autopilot-loop SKILL.md
- [ ] .gitignore rules: STATUS.md ignored, everything else committed
- [ ] config.md template with all fields
- [ ] evaluation.json schema matches evaluator spike output
- [ ] Initialization creates directory and config.md from kickoff input
close_reason: Created .autopilot/.gitignore (ignores STATUS.md) and .autopilot/config.md.template with full config schema and evaluation.json schema documented in comment block

title: /autopilot entry command
description:
## Context
The /autopilot command is the user-facing entry point. It asks 2-3 interactive questions, writes the config, and hands off to the autopilot-loop controller for fully autonomous execution.

## File Scope
- `.claude/commands/autopilot.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Interactive kickoff: asks for idea, success criteria, optional constraints
- [ ] Writes .autopilot/config.md with structured input
- [ ] Sets default thresholds (goal=85, delta=5, max_iterations=5)
- [ ] Allows user to override thresholds if desired
- [ ] Invokes autopilot-loop skill after config is written
- [ ] Clear user-facing messaging: "Starting autonomous loop. I'll handle everything from here."
- [ ] No frontmatter (command convention)
close_reason: /autopilot command created with interactive kickoff and autonomous handoff

title: Proposer agent definition
description:
## Context
The Proposer agent expands scope during the adversarial discussion phase. It generates features, explores ambition, and argues for additions.

## Spike Learnings (from .spikes/discussion-protocol/)
- Must use structured message format with [KEEP], [ADD], [CUT], [MODIFY] tagged items
- Must include ## Confidence, ## Concessions, ## Open Questions sections
- Communicates via Agent Mail thread `discussion-{iteration}`
- Reads challenger's prior message before responding

## File Scope
- `.claude/agents/proposer.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] YAML frontmatter: name, description, model (sonnet for cost efficiency)
- [ ] Instructions to read Agent Mail thread for context
- [ ] Strict message format template embedded in instructions
- [ ] Role: expand scope, propose features, argue for additions
- [ ] Must include Concessions section (critical for convergence)
- [ ] Must read prior iteration evaluator feedback when provided in seed message

title: Autopilot-loop controller skill
description:
## Context
The Autopilot-loop skill is the central controller. It sequences all phases (discuss → plan → build → evaluate) and manages the iteration loop with triple stop conditions.

## Architecture
For each iteration:
1. Invoke discussion skill (produces .autopilot/iteration-N/discussion-result.md)
2. Invoke /planning skill with discussion output as input (produces beads)
3. Invoke /orchestrator skill (workers build beads with TDD)
4. Spawn evaluator agent (produces .autopilot/iteration-N/evaluation.json)
5. Check stop conditions: max_iterations → goal_threshold → diminishing_returns
6. Write .autopilot/iteration-N/summary.md
7. Update .autopilot/STATUS.md
8. Loop or exit

## Spike Learnings
- From evaluator spike: controller can override verdict (e.g., always continue if iteration < 2)
- From discussion spike: seed message for iteration 2+ must include evaluator feedback
- From discovery: recipe skill pattern for phase chaining, session-state for crash recovery

## File Scope
- `.claude/skills/autopilot-loop/SKILL.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] YAML frontmatter: name, description, model (opus)
- [ ] Full iteration loop documented with all 8 steps
- [ ] Integration points: invokes discussion skill, planning skill, orchestrator skill
- [ ] Stop condition logic with threshold defaults and check order
- [ ] State management: reads/writes .autopilot/ directory
- [ ] Error handling: what happens if a phase fails mid-iteration
- [ ] Iteration context passing: evaluator output feeds into next discussion seed
- [ ] Final summary: writes .autopilot/FINAL.md with overall results when loop terminates
close_reason: Controller skill created with full loop logic, stop conditions, error recovery

title: Evaluator agent definition
description:
## Context
The Evaluator agent scores each build iteration to determine if the loop should continue or stop.

## Spike Learnings (from .spikes/evaluator-scoring/)
- 4-dimension rubric: functionality (40%), completeness (25%), quality (20%), runnability (15%)
- Each dimension scored 0-100, overall = weighted sum
- Outputs structured JSON with dimensions, overall_score, delta, criteria_checklist, verdict
- Threshold defaults: goal_threshold=85, delta_threshold=5, max_iterations=5
- Check order: max iterations → goal threshold → diminishing returns
- criteria_checklist feeds next discussion with explicit unmet items
- Should run tests if possible for runnability scoring

## File Scope
- `.claude/agents/evaluator.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] YAML frontmatter: name, description, model (opus for reasoning quality)
- [ ] Evaluation procedure: load config → inspect artifacts → score dimensions → compute delta → emit verdict
- [ ] Scoring rubric with all 4 dimensions and weights documented
- [ ] Output JSON schema fully specified in instructions
- [ ] Must read .autopilot/config.md for success criteria
- [ ] Must read previous evaluation.json for delta comparison
- [ ] Must inspect actual code/tests, not just descriptions
close_reason: Created .claude/agents/evaluator.md with YAML frontmatter (name=evaluator, model=claude-opus-4-5), full 9-step evaluation procedure, 4-dimension scoring rubric with weights, exact output JSON schema, stop condition check order, and bash-based artifact inspection
